conversation.content:
Reconcile the following columns. Document has 13 pages. Sources are anonymous (A and B).

COLUMNS:

---
Column 1: Treatment Class
Definition: What is the categorization or classification of the treatment or therapy received by participants in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="triplet therapy (androgen deprivation therapy, docetaxel, and abiraterone)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The summary section on page 1 describes the combination of androgen deprivation therapy, docetaxel, and abiraterone as 'triplet therapy'. The 'Added value of this study' section on page 2 further specifies it as 'triple systemic therapy-consisting of ADT, docetaxel, and a second-generation androgen "
  B: value="Androgen deprivation therapy, docetaxel, and abiraterone plus prednisone (second-generation androgen signalling inhibitor)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Found in the summary and introduction on page 1, describing the components of the triple systemic therapy."

---
Column 2: Trial
Definition: What name of the clinical study or experiment being referenced?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="PEACE-1" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The trial name 'PEACE-1' is explicitly mentioned in the title of the paper."
  B: value="PEACE-1" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Clearly stated in the title of the article on page 1."

---
Column 3: Type of Therapy
Definition: What is the description of the type of treatment or intervention provided to participants in the clinical trial?. If reported, provide the value; otherwise use 'Not reported'.
  A: value="standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abi" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The 'Methods' section on page 1 details the different treatment arms, including 'standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily"
  B: value="Triple systemic therapy (androgen deprivation therapy, docetaxel, and abiraterone plus prednisone)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Described as 'triple systemic therapy' in the interpretation on page 1 and further detailed in the summary and introduction."

---
Column 4: Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treatment
Definition: What percentage of participants in the treatment arm of the clinical trial who received docetaxel before or during the trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="355 (60.9%)" | page=5 | modality=figure | reasoning="Figure 1 shows that in the '583 in SOC (with or without radiotherapy) plus abiraterone groups', '355 included in the ADT with docetaxel population for efficacy analysis'. This means 355 out of 583 patients in the abiraterone (treatment) arm received docetaxel. (355/583)*100 = 60.89%."
  B: value="355 (60.9%)" | page=5 | modality=figure | reasoning="From Figure 1 on page 5, summing patients who received docetaxel in the 'SOC plus abiraterone' and 'SOC plus abiraterone plus radiotherapy' groups (177 + 178 = 355) out of the total patients in these groups (292 + 291 = 583)."

---
Column 5: Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Control
Definition: What number and percentage of participants who received docetaxel before or during the trial in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="355 (60.3%)" | page=5 | modality=figure | reasoning="Figure 1 shows that in the '589 in SOC (with or without radiotherapy) without abiraterone groups', '355 included in the ADT with docetaxel population for efficacy analysis'. This means 355 out of 589 patients in the control arm received docetaxel. (355/589)*100 = 60.27%."
  B: value="355 (60.3%)" | page=5 | modality=figure | reasoning="From Figure 1 on page 5, summing patients who received docetaxel in the 'SOC' and 'SOC plus radiotherapy' groups (178 + 177 = 355) out of the total patients in these groups (296 + 293 = 589)."

---
Column 6: Median Age (years) | Treatment
Definition: What median age in years of participants included in the treatment arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="67 (IQR 61-72)" | page=6 | modality=table | reasoning="Table 1, 'Age, years' row, 'Median' sub-row, under 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=583)' column, reports '67 (61-72)'."
  B: value="67 (IQR 61-72)" | page=6 | modality=table | reasoning="From Table 1 on page 6, under 'Age, years Median' for 'SOC plus abiraterone groups'."

---
Column 7: Median Age (years) | Control
Definition: What the median age of participants included in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="66 (IQR 59-72)" | page=6 | modality=table | reasoning="Table 1, 'Age, years' row, 'Median' sub-row, under 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=589)' column, reports '66 (59-72)'."
  B: value="66 (IQR 59-72)" | page=6 | modality=table | reasoning="From Table 1 on page 6, under 'Age, years Median' for 'SOC without abiraterone groups'."

---
Column 8: Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Treatment
Definition: What is the median duration in months that participants in the treatment arm of the trial received on-treatment therapy? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document provides 'median abiraterone treatment duration before discontinuation' for specific subgroups (ADT with docetaxel population and ADT without docetaxel population) within the abiraterone arm, but not for the entire abiraterone treatment arm (n=583). The question asks for 'Median On-Trea"
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document provides the duration of abiraterone treatment and the number of docetaxel cycles, but not a single median on-treatment duration for the entire regimen in the treatment arm."

---
Column 9: Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Control
Definition: What is the median duration in months of participants receiving treatment in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The median on-treatment duration for the control arm (SOC without abiraterone) is not explicitly reported in the document."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document provides the number of docetaxel cycles, but not a single median on-treatment duration for the entire regimen in the control arm."

---
Column 10: No. of Deaths - N | Treatment
Definition: What the number of participants who died in the treatment arm of the clinical trial?.
  A: value="228" | page=5 | modality=figure | reasoning="Figure 1, under '583 in SOC (with or without radiotherapy) plus abiraterone groups', states '228 deceased at data cutoff'."
  B: value="228" | page=5 | modality=figure | reasoning="From Figure 1 on page 5, '228 deceased at data cutoff' for 'SOC (with or without radiotherapy) plus abiraterone groups'."

---
Column 11: No. of Deaths - N | Control
Definition: What number of deaths that occurred in the control arm of the clinical trial?.
  A: value="268" | page=5 | modality=figure | reasoning="Figure 1, under '589 in SOC (with or without radiotherapy) without abiraterone groups', states '268 deceased at data cutoff'."
  B: value="268" | page=5 | modality=figure | reasoning="From Figure 1 on page 5, '268 deceased at data cutoff' for 'SOC (with or without radiotherapy) without abiraterone groups'."

---
Column 12: PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment
Definition: What the hazard ratio with confidence intervals of progression-free survival (PFS) of all comparisons in all participants, regardless of metastasis type or volume, in the treatment arm of the trial? If reported, include the rate (percentage) and any accompanying details. If multiple arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document reports median radiographic progression-free survival duration (4.46 years) and hazard ratios (HR 0.54) for the overall population, but not a percentage rate for PFS."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document provides median radiographic progression-free survival and hazard ratios, but not a specific PFS rate (percentage) for the treatment arm."

---
Column 13: PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control
Definition: What hazard ratio with confidence intervals of Progression-Free Survival (PFS) of all comparisons in all participants in the control arm of the trial? If reported, include the rate (percentage) and any accompanying details. If multiple arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document reports median radiographic progression-free survival duration (2.22 years) and hazard ratios (HR 0.54) for the overall population, but not a percentage rate for PFS."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document provides median radiographic progression-free survival and hazard ratios, but not a specific PFS rate (percentage) for the control arm."

---
Column 14: TTPSA (mo) | Treatment
Definition: What time To Progression of Serum Androgen (in months) in the treatment arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document mentions PSA response rate and CRPC-free survival (which involves PSA criteria) but does not report a specific 'Time To Progression of Serum Androgen (TTPSA)' as a distinct endpoint with a median value."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="TTPSA (Time To Progression of Serum Androgen) was not reported in the provided document pages."

---
Column 15: TTPSA (mo) | Control
Definition: What time to Progressive Serum Androgen (TTPSA) in months for participants in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include the value; use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document mentions PSA response rate and CRPC-free survival (which involves PSA criteria) but does not report a specific 'Time To Progression of Serum Androgen (TTPSA)' as a distinct endpoint with a median value."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="TTPSA (Time To Progression of Serum Androgen) was not reported in the provided document pages."


Submit verification for easy columns first. For disputed columns, use get_page then submit.

response.formatted_chunks:
[Page 1]
## Page 1

Articles



Abiraterone plus prednisone added to androgen deprivation therapy and docetaxel in de novo metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (PEACE-1): a multicentre, open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2 x 2 factorial design



<::logo: CrossMark
CrossMark
The logo features a stylized 'W' with an arrow, a cursor icon, and a bookmark symbol, all in a deep red color.::>



Karim Fizazi, Stéphanie Foulon, Joan Carles, Guilhem Roubaud, Ray McDermott, Aude Fléchon, Bertrand Tombal, Stéphane Supiot,
Dominik Berthold, Philippe Ronchin, Gabriel Kacso, Gwenaëlle Gravis, Fabio Calabro, Jean-François Berdah, Ali Hasbini, Marlon Silva,
Antoine Thiery-Vuillemin, Igor Latorzeff, Loïc Mourey, Brigitte Laguerre, Sophie Abadie-Lacourtoisie, Etienne Martin, Claude El Kouri,
Anne Escande, Alvar Rosello, Nicolas Magne, Friederike Schlurmann, Frank Priou, Marie-Eve Chand-Fouche, Salvador Villà Freixa,
Muhammad Jamaluddin, Isabelle Rieger, Alberto Bossi, on behalf of the PEACE-1 investigators*



## Summary

**Background** Current standard of care for metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer supplements androgen deprivation therapy with either docetaxel, second-generation hormonal therapy, or radiotherapy. We aimed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of abiraterone plus prednisone, with or without radiotherapy, in addition to standard of care.



Methods We conducted an open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2x2 factorial design (PEACE-1) at 77 hospitals across Belgium, France, Ireland, Italy, Romania, Spain, and Switzerland. Eligible patients were male, aged 18 years or older, with histologically confirmed or cytologically confirmed de novo metastatic prostate adenocarcinoma, and an Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status of 0–1 (or 2 due to bone pain).
Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone. Neither the investigators nor the patients were masked to treatment allocation. The coprimary endpoints were radiographic progression-free survival and overall survival. Abiraterone efficacy was first assessed in the overall population and then in the population who received androgen deprivation therapy with docetaxel as standard of care (population of interest). This study is ongoing and is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov, NCT01957436.



Findings Between Nov 27, 2013, and Dec 20, 2018, 1173 patients were enrolled (one patient subsequently withdrew consent for analysis of his data) and assigned to receive standard of care (n=296), standard of care plus radiotherapy (n=293), standard of care plus abiraterone (n=292), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone (n=291). Median follow-up was 3.5 years (IQR 2.8–4.6) for radiographic progression-free survival and 4.4 years (3.5–5.4) for overall survival. Adjusted Cox regression modelling revealed no interaction between abiraterone and radiotherapy, enabling the pooled analysis of abiraterone efficacy. In the overall population, patients assigned to receive abiraterone (n=583) had longer radiographic progression-free survival (hazard ratio [HR] 0.54, 99.9% CI 0.41–0.71; p<0.0001) and overall survival (0.82, 95.1% CI 0.69–0.98; p=0.030) than patients who did not receive abiraterone (n=589). In the androgen deprivation therapy with docetaxel population (n=355 in both with abiraterone and without abiraterone groups), the HRs were consistent (radiographic progression-free survival 0.50, 99.9% CI 0.34–0.71; p<0.0001; overall survival 0.75, 95.1% CI 0.59–0.95; p=0.017). In the androgen deprivation therapy with docetaxel population, grade 3 or worse adverse events occurred in 217 (63%) of 347 patients who received abiraterone and 181 (52%) of 350 who did not; hypertension had the largest difference in occurrence (76 [22%] patients and 45 [13%], respectively). Addition of abiraterone to androgen deprivation therapy plus docetaxel did not increase the rates of neutropenia, febrile neutropenia, fatigue, or neuropathy compared with androgen deprivation therapy plus docetaxel alone.



**Interpretation** Combining androgen deprivation therapy, docetaxel, and abiraterone in de novo metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer improved overall survival and radiographic progression-free survival with a modest increase in toxicity, mostly hypertension. This triplet therapy could become a standard of care for these patients.



Funding Janssen-Cilag, Ipsen, Sanofi, and the French Government.
